author: Muhammad Din, Fauq
title: Imtihan Pass karne ka Gur
gloss: A guide to students for ensuring success in examinations.
notes: '(Designed for educational purposes).' printed above entry. 'The Secret of Passing Examinations' — exam-cram genre in its purest form, published by Master Jagat Singh (the agriculture-catechism author of p23–24); likely the Lahore littérateur Muhammad Din Fauq.

author: Muhammad Shams-ud-din Shaiq, Munshi
title: Shafi-us-Sanat
gloss: A speech on the advantages of learning handicrafts, delivered by the author at the opening ceremony of the Edward Muhammadan Technical School, Lahore, with a poem by Munshi Allah Yar Khan, Jogi.
notes: Technical-education promotion: a printed inaugural speech for the Edward Muhammadan Technical School, issued by the 'Society of Patrons of the Skilled' — craft-uplift discourse in Muslim reformism.

author: Mukand Lal, Pandit
title: Taskhir-i-Yakhni
gloss: A book of charms and amulets.
notes: 168-page occult manual (taskhir = subjugation of spirits; title likely Taskhir-i-Yakshni, printed 'Yakhni') published by Hakim Ram Kishan — his fourth appearance this quarter, now in the charms market.

author: Nand Lal, Lala
title: Tamaku-noshi, Hissa-i-Duwum
gloss: A tract describing the evil effects of tobacco smoking, Part II.
notes: Temperance Society of Amritsar extending from liquor to tobacco; same society and date as the Punjabi Temperance Git (p16 s302) — coordinated multi-language, multi-vice campaign, consecutive regs 1347–1349.

author: Nand Lal, Lala
title: Tamaku-noshi, Hissa-i-Siwum
notes: Author dash-continuation of s174; catalog gives only 'Hissa-i-Siwum, Part III'.

title: Pioneer Gentleman Diary Kalan
notes: English-titled 'Pioneer Gentleman Diary' (large size) at 3,000 copies — anglophone self-fashioning as a stationery commodity; pairs with s177.

author: Prem Anand, Babu
title: Hindustan men Khaufnak Zulm ka Nazara
gloss: A book dealing with such subjects, as cow-killing, widow-remarriage, Swadeshi, etc.
notes: 'A View of the Terrible Oppression in Hindustan' — cow protection + widow remarriage + Swadeshi in one Arya-press tract; the registrar's own list of its 'subjects' maps the agitational agenda of 1910.
